activity_title: eBay blank-title root cause proven and fixed - titles live in order_item_info, not listing_data
activity_summary: Read-only root-cause investigation into blank eBay titles in the Mismatch Candidates section. Traced all 21 blank-title SKUs through every title source in the database (not the skill docs): listing_data by SKU and by item_id returned no title; public.order_item_info.oii_item_title had a clean title for all 21 (100% coverage, vs listing_data 16/68 by SKU, 5/68 by item_id). Root cause: the implementation sourced titles from listing_data per the skill docs, but eBay titles actually live in order_item_info, a table the docs never mention. Filled the 21 blanks with a structurally-verified, title-only edit - only the title field changed; refund total 1664.07, NAD total 70, candidate counts, rankings and badges were byte-identical before and after.
evidence_refs: 21 blank eBay rows; order_item_info coverage 21/21 (counts 2-188); listing_data 16/68 by SKU, 5/68 by item_id; refund 1664.07 and NAD 70 unchanged; only 21 title fields (index 3) changed
reusable_pattern: To recover a missing display attribute, trace the value through every candidate table by both keys (SKU and stable id) before trusting the documented source; the real store may be an order-line table the schema docs omit.
validation_rule: A title-only fix must change only the title field: assert every diff is at the title index, no non-blank value is overwritten, and refund/count/ranking/badge aggregates are byte-identical before and after.
gap_or_risk: The skill/TABLE docs did not name order_item_info as the eBay title source; documentation is incomplete versus the live schema.
next_action: None for eBay titles (resolved); keep order_item_info as the eBay title source of record.

activity_title: Amazon blank titles recovered by ASIN; discovered dual-backend order_management_copy
activity_summary: Applied the same read-only method to 9 blank Amazon titles. Root cause differs from eBay: the SKUs carry variant suffixes (-SA, -UM, -MP, -SN) that miss the listing_data SKU, but the ASIN matches - all 9 recover keyed by ASIN (ref_id). 8 of the 9 exist only on wrong_sku=1 rows (the US-marketplace listing for the same ASIN); the titles are correct product descriptions. Per user decision, filled all 9 by ASIN (HRBK1 has no English title anywhere, so it took a clean-ASCII Italian title). Critical infrastructure finding: the MCP connection resolves to a different database than earlier in the session - the temp_user backend has order_item_info, the postgres backend has only listing_data - two databases both named order_management_copy.
evidence_refs: Amazon 9 blanks, all 9 recover by ASIN; only LDST45E2743PK has wrong_sku=0 (UK+Ireland), other 8 wrong_sku=1 US; refund 4642.33 and badge count 3 unchanged; backend probe: order_item_info ABSENT on temp_user, PRESENT earlier via MCP
reusable_pattern: When a SKU-keyed title is blank but the row has a stable ASIN/item_id, re-key the lookup by that stable id; treat wrong_sku=1 (flagged) rows as a last-resort title source and surface the trust trade-off to the user before using them.
validation_rule: Before trusting any DB result across a session, verify current_user, current_database and to_regclass for the tables you depend on; a connection behind a proxy can resolve to different backends.
gap_or_risk: MCP and the refresh .env resolve to two different databases both named order_management_copy; they are not interchangeable and must be reconciled. temp_user password exposure from D01 still open.
next_action: Pin one authoritative order_management_copy backend for both MCP and the refresh; rotate the exposed temp_user password.

activity_title: Dashboard verified, auto-vs-Claude output compared, daily 10:00 cron configured
activity_summary: Read-only discovery of the refresh architecture: refresh_dashboard.py rewrites only const DATA and const GENERATED_AT via replace_data_block; const MISMATCH is a static snapshot. Verified the Mismatch Candidates section (tab t5, mm* functions, Amazon/eBay sub-tabs, Review Candidate badge, listing links, empty-state) is already present, so nothing was appended (appending would duplicate). Ran the refresh: JS node --check OK, only DATA and GENERATED_AT changed, const MISMATCH byte-identical. Compared the automatic output against the Claude-generated version: identical except const DATA and GENERATED_AT, whose delta is the 3-month window sliding one day. Configured a daily 10:00 cron (0 10 * * *) as the only RRHD entry, preserving the two existing cron jobs.
evidence_refs: refresh 2109 rows exit 0; MISMATCH sha 6f35a09c identical pre/post; only lines 715-716 (DATA, GENERATED_AT) differ; window slid 2026-04-15 to 04-16; crontab RRHD entries = 1
reusable_pattern: Prove a refresh changed only data by hashing the const DATA/MISMATCH substrings and diffing line numbers, not by eyeballing; install cron idempotently by filtering any prior matching line before appending so re-runs never duplicate.
validation_rule: A daily refresh must change only data plus timestamp: const MISMATCH hash unchanged, structure fingerprint equal, JS still parses (node --check), and exactly the DATA/GENERATED_AT lines differ.
gap_or_risk: Before the D03-A04 fix, const MISMATCH never refreshed - the section silently aged against live data (GAP-4 carried from D02).
next_action: Addressed in D03-A04 (wired MISMATCH into the refresh).

activity_title: Wired Mismatch Candidates into the daily refresh with deterministic MISMATCH regeneration
activity_summary: Root-caused why the Mismatch section never updates: refresh_dashboard.py never executed the two nad_* queries, and replace_data_block only swaps const DATA and GENERATED_AT; additionally the refresh backend lacks order_item_info so titles cannot be re-sourced there. Smallest fix that preserves functionality: in SQL, added a NAD-only allocated nad_refund to nad_ebay_candidates, made eBay SKU attribution deterministic (ORDER BY o.sku on the LIMIT 1 lateral), and added full tie-breakers to both listing-context DISTINCT ON CTEs (the Amazon one silently flipped title and currency between runs); in Python, added read_const_object, replace_const_object and build_mismatch_rows to regenerate const MISMATCH, with a validated title carry-forward (title stable by SKU then ASIN/item_id) because order_item_info is absent on the refresh backend. Proven deterministic: two consecutive runs produce a byte-identical MISMATCH; const DATA unchanged; only the MISMATCH line differs; backup and logging intact.
evidence_refs: refresh now runs 6 queries (nad_amazon 177, nad_ebay 69); MISMATCH updated amazon 175 to 177, ebay refund 1664.07 to 1716.45; determinism run1==run2 sha 5590a930; 2 remaining eBay blanks are genuinely-new SKUs; DATA sha df9989f8 unchanged; exit 0
reusable_pattern: To make an embedded snapshot refresh safely: regenerate it from SQL, swap only its const block (brace-balanced), guard against empty overwrites, make every DISTINCT ON / LIMIT 1 deterministic with full tie-breakers, and carry forward values the refresh DB cannot re-source.
validation_rule: A regenerated data block must be deterministic: two consecutive refreshes with unchanged inputs must produce a byte-identical block; otherwise a DISTINCT ON or LIMIT 1 lacks a full tie-break and will reshuffle daily.
gap_or_risk: eBay titles for genuinely-new SKUs stay blank because the refresh backend has no order_item_info; repointing the refresh at the backend that has it would auto-populate them.
next_action: Commit the SQL and refresh changes; optionally repoint the refresh .env at the backend exposing order_item_info so new eBay titles populate.
